section.heading: Low-income requirement and income targeting

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: Housing shall qualify for affordable housing for purposes of this subchapter only if—

paragraph.chapeau: each dwelling unit in the housing—

subparagraph.content: in the case of rental housing, is made available for occupancy only by a family that is a low-income family at the time of the initial occupancy of that family of that unit; and
in the case of housing for homeownership, is made available for purchase only by a family that is a low-income family at the time of purchase; and

paragraph.chapeau: each dwelling unit in the housing will remain affordable, according to binding commitments satisfactory to the Secretary, for—

subparagraph.content: the remaining useful life of the property (as determined by the Secretary) without regard to the term of the mortgage or to transfer of ownership; or

subparagraph.chapeau: such other period as the Secretary determines is the longest feasible period of time consistent with sound economics and the purposes of this subchapter, except upon a foreclosure by a lender (or upon other transfer in lieu of foreclosure) if that action—

clause.chapeau: recognizes any contractual or legal rights of any public agency, nonprofit sponsor, or other person or entity to take an action that would—

subclause.content: avoid termination of low-income affordability, in the case of foreclosure; or
transfer ownership in lieu of foreclosure; and

clause.content: is not for the purpose of avoiding low-income affordability restrictions, as determined by the Secretary.

content.p: Notwithstanding subsection (a), housing assistance pursuant to section 4228(a)(2)(B) of this title shall be considered affordable housing for purposes of this subchapter.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 104–330, title VIII, § 813, as added Pub. L. 106–568, title II, § 203, Dec. 27, 2000, 114 Stat. 2888, and Pub. L. 106–569, title V, § 513, Dec. 27, 2000, 114 Stat. 2982.)

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Pub. L. 106–568, § 203, and Pub. L. 106–569, § 513, enacted identical sections 813 of Pub. L. 104–330. This section is based on the text of section 813 of Pub. L. 104–330, as added by Pub. L. 106–569, § 513.
